title: Define AnimationStep and VisualMutation type system
description: Create the core type definitions for algorithm visualization steps: AnimationStep (swap, compare, highlight, set, partition, merge) and VisualMutation (index-based state changes). These types drive every algorithm visualizer.
acceptanceCriteria: AnimationStep type covers swap, compare, highlight, set, partition, and merge mutations
VisualMutation type tracks index-based state changes with before/after values
Types are exported from src/lib/algorithms/types.ts

title: Implement PlaybackController class
description: Build the PlaybackController that manages step-through playback of algorithm animations: play, pause, step forward/back, speed control, and reset. Consumes AnimationStep arrays from generators.
acceptanceCriteria: Supports play, pause, step-forward, step-back, and reset operations
Configurable playback speed (0.25x to 4x)
Emits current step index and completion status via callbacks

title: Implement Bubble Sort step generator
description: Create a generator function that yields AnimationStep arrays for Bubble Sort. Each step highlights the comparison pair, marks swaps, and tracks the sorted boundary shrinking from the right.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields correct AnimationStep sequence for any input array
Steps include compare, swap, and sorted-boundary markers
O(n^2) worst-case steps match expected Bubble Sort behavior

title: Implement Merge Sort step generator
description: Create a generator function that yields AnimationStep arrays for Merge Sort. Visualizes the recursive split phase and the merge phase with auxiliary array operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields correct split and merge steps for any input array
Steps clearly distinguish split boundaries and merge operations
Recursive depth is tracked for visual indentation

title: Implement Quick Sort (Lomuto partition) step generator
description: Create a generator function for Quick Sort using Lomuto partitioning. Steps show pivot selection, partition boundary movement, element comparisons, and swaps.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator uses Lomuto partition scheme with last-element pivot
Steps highlight pivot, partition boundary, comparison element, and swaps
Recursive calls produce correctly nested step sequences

title: Implement Heap Sort step generator
description: Create a generator function for Heap Sort. Steps visualize the heapify phase building a max-heap and the extraction phase swapping root to sorted position.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields heapify-down sift steps during build-heap phase
Extraction phase swaps root element and re-heapifies
Parent-child index relationships are annotated in steps

title: Implement Insertion Sort step generator
description: Create a generator function for Insertion Sort. Steps show the key element being extracted, comparison scan leftward, shifting, and final insertion.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields compare and shift steps as key moves leftward
Sorted boundary grows from left with each outer iteration
Key insertion position is highlighted

title: Implement Selection Sort step generator
description: Create a generator function for Selection Sort. Steps highlight the scanning phase finding the minimum and the swap placing it at the sorted boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields scan-compare steps and a final swap per outer iteration
Current minimum candidate is tracked and highlighted throughout the scan
Sorted boundary grows from the left correctly

title: Build ArrayVisualizer with spring animations
description: Create the SortingVisualizer React component that renders an array as animated bars. Uses spring physics (framer-motion or react-spring) for smooth swap, compare-highlight, and sorted-state transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Bars animate position swaps with spring physics (no teleporting)
Compare, swap, and sorted states use distinct color coding
Component accepts AnimationStep and renders the corresponding mutation

title: Build AlgorithmPanel sidebar with controls
description: Create the sidebar panel for the Algorithm Visualizer module. Includes algorithm category selector, algorithm picker dropdown, input size slider, speed control, and play/pause/step buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar lists algorithm categories (Sorting, Graph, Tree, DP, String)
Play/pause/step-forward/step-back/reset controls wired to PlaybackController
Speed slider adjusts playback rate from 0.25x to 4x

title: Wire AlgorithmModule layout (sidebar + canvas + properties + bottom panel)
description: Compose the full AlgorithmModule page layout: sidebar on the left with controls, main canvas area with the active visualizer, properties panel on the right with complexity info, and bottom panel for code/pseudocode.
acceptanceCriteria: Four-panel layout renders correctly with resizable boundaries
Switching algorithms swaps the canvas visualizer component
Properties panel displays time/space complexity for the selected algorithm

title: Create SORTING_ALGORITHMS catalog registry
description: Build a static catalog object that registers all sorting algorithms with metadata: name, key, complexity, description, generator function reference, and category tag. Used by the sidebar picker and properties panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Catalog includes all 6 initial sorting algorithms (Bubble, Merge, Quick, Heap, Insertion, Selection)
Each entry has name, key, timeComplexity, spaceComplexity, and generatorFn
Catalog is exported from src/lib/algorithms/sorting/index.ts

title: Implement Shell Sort step generator
description: Create a generator function for Shell Sort. Steps visualize the gap sequence shrinking and the gapped insertion sort passes, highlighting which elements are being compared across the gap.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator uses a standard gap sequence (Knuth or Shell's original)
Steps highlight the current gap size and elements being compared across the gap
Final pass (gap=1) is equivalent to insertion sort and produces correct output

title: Implement Counting Sort step generator with histogram visualization
description: Create a generator for Counting Sort. Steps visualize the count array (histogram) being built, the prefix-sum accumulation, and the output array being filled by placing elements at their counted positions.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields steps for count-array construction, prefix-sum, and output placement
Histogram bars are rendered alongside the input/output arrays
Works correctly for non-negative integer inputs within a configurable range

title: Implement Radix Sort LSD step generator with bucket visualization
description: Create a generator for Least-Significant-Digit Radix Sort. Steps visualize processing each digit position, distributing elements into buckets 0-9, and collecting them back.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator processes digits from least significant to most significant
Steps show elements being distributed into 10 buckets and collected back
Bucket state is included in each step for visualization of the 0-9 distribution

title: Implement Radix Sort MSD step generator with recursion tree
description: Create a generator for Most-Significant-Digit Radix Sort. Steps visualize top-down digit processing with recursive subdivision of buckets, displayed as a recursion tree showing how subarrays are partitioned.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator processes digits from most significant to least significant
Steps include recursion-tree metadata showing which subarray and digit position is active
Produces correct sorted output for variable-length integer inputs

title: Implement Bucket Sort step generator
description: Create a generator for Bucket Sort. Steps visualize distributing elements into k buckets based on value range, sorting each bucket individually (using insertion sort), and concatenating the results.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator distributes elements into configurable number of buckets
Steps show per-bucket insertion sort passes
Final concatenation phase collects sorted buckets into the output array

title: Implement Tim Sort step generator with run detection and galloping merge
description: Create a generator for Tim Sort. Steps visualize run detection (natural ascending/descending runs), run reversal if descending, minrun-based insertion sort on short runs, and galloping-mode merge passes.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator detects natural runs and enforces minrun with insertion sort
Steps show run boundaries, reversal of descending runs, and merge operations
Galloping mode is triggered and visualized when one run consistently wins comparisons

title: Implement Cocktail Shaker Sort step generator
description: Create a generator for Cocktail Shaker Sort (bidirectional Bubble Sort). Steps visualize the forward pass bubbling the largest element right, then the backward pass bubbling the smallest element left, with shrinking boundaries on both ends.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator alternates forward and backward comparison passes
Steps show both left and right sorted boundaries shrinking inward
Produces identical final result to Bubble Sort but with fewer passes on nearly-sorted data

title: Implement Comb Sort step generator
description: Create a generator for Comb Sort. Steps visualize the gap shrinking by the shrink factor (1.3), comparing and swapping elements separated by the current gap, converging to a final Bubble Sort pass when gap reaches 1.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator uses shrink factor 1.3 and shrinks gap each iteration
Steps highlight the current gap size and the pair of elements being compared
Final gap-1 pass is equivalent to Bubble Sort

title: Build graph visualization renderer with d3-force layout
description: Create a GraphVisualizer React component that renders nodes as circles and edges as lines/arrows using a d3-force simulation for automatic layout. Supports directed/undirected graphs, weighted edges, and node coloring for algorithm state.
acceptanceCriteria: Nodes render as circles with labels using d3-force for positioning
Edges render as lines (undirected) or arrows (directed) with optional weight labels
Node and edge colors update reactively based on algorithm step state (visited, current, frontier)

title: Build graph input editor (click-to-create, drag-to-connect)
description: Create an interactive graph editor overlay for the GraphVisualizer. Users can click the canvas to create nodes, drag between nodes to create edges, and set edge weights via a popup. Supports undo/redo.
acceptanceCriteria: Click on empty canvas creates a new node at that position
Dragging from one node to another creates an edge (with optional weight prompt)
Delete key removes selected node or edge; Ctrl+Z undoes the last action

title: Implement BFS step generator
description: Create a generator function for Breadth-First Search. Steps visualize the queue state, node discovery order, level transitions, and edge classification (tree edge vs cross edge).
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields steps showing queue contents, current node, and discovered neighbors
Steps distinguish tree edges from cross edges
Level boundaries are tracked for level-order visualization

title: Implement DFS step generator
description: Create a generator function for Depth-First Search. Steps visualize the stack state, node discovery/finish times, edge classification (tree, back, forward, cross), and backtracking.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields steps showing stack contents, current node, and recursive exploration
Steps classify edges as tree, back, forward, or cross
Discovery and finish timestamps are recorded for each node

title: Implement Dijkstra's algorithm step generator
description: Create a generator for Dijkstra's shortest path. Steps visualize the priority queue, distance table updates, edge relaxation decisions, and the shortest-path tree growing from the source.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields steps showing priority queue extraction, neighbor relaxation, and distance updates
Distance table is included in step state for the properties panel
Shortest-path tree edges are distinguished from non-tree edges

title: Implement Bellman-Ford step generator
description: Create a generator for Bellman-Ford shortest paths. Steps visualize each of the V-1 relaxation passes over all edges, distance table updates, and the final negative-cycle detection pass.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields steps for each of V-1 edge-relaxation passes with distance updates
Negative cycle detection pass is included and flags any negative cycle found
Distance table state is emitted with each step for side-panel display

title: Implement A* Search step generator
description: Create a generator for A* Search. Steps visualize the open set (priority queue), closed set, g/h/f score updates, heuristic evaluation, and the optimal path reconstruction.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields steps showing open-set extraction, neighbor evaluation with g+h=f scores
Heuristic function is configurable (Manhattan, Euclidean, or custom)
Path reconstruction step traces back from goal to source via parent pointers

title: Implement Topological Sort (Kahn's algorithm) step generator
description: Create a generator for Kahn's topological sort. Steps visualize in-degree computation, zero-in-degree queue processing, node removal and neighbor in-degree decrements, and cycle detection if the graph is not a DAG.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields steps showing in-degree table, zero-degree queue, and node processing order
Steps decrement neighbor in-degrees and enqueue newly zero-degree nodes
Detects and reports cycles if not all nodes are processed

title: Implement Kruskal's MST step generator
description: Create a generator for Kruskal's Minimum Spanning Tree. Steps visualize edge sorting by weight, union-find operations for cycle detection, and MST edge selection with running total weight.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator sorts edges by weight and processes them in order
Steps show union-find membership, cycle detection, and edge accept/reject decisions
Running MST weight total is tracked in step state

title: Implement Prim's MST step generator
description: Create a generator for Prim's Minimum Spanning Tree. Steps visualize the growing MST frontier, priority queue of cut edges, minimum-edge selection, and the MST tree expanding one node at a time.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields steps showing the MST set, cut edges in the priority queue, and minimum selection
Steps highlight the chosen edge and newly added node
Running MST weight total is tracked in step state

title: Implement Floyd-Warshall step generator with NxN matrix visualization
description: Create a generator for Floyd-Warshall all-pairs shortest paths. Steps visualize the NxN distance matrix being updated as each intermediate vertex k is considered, highlighting the cell being relaxed and the triangle inequality check.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator iterates over intermediate vertices k and yields matrix-update steps
Steps highlight the current cell (i,j), the path via k cells (i,k) and (k,j), and whether relaxation occurs
Final matrix contains correct all-pairs shortest path distances

title: Implement Tarjan's SCC step generator
description: Create a generator for Tarjan's Strongly Connected Components algorithm. Steps visualize the DFS traversal with discovery/low-link values, stack operations, and SCC identification when a root node is found.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields DFS steps with discovery index and low-link value updates
Stack state is included in each step for visualization
SCCs are identified and highlighted as groups when a root node (disc == low) is popped

title: Build tree visualization renderer with hierarchical layout
description: Create a TreeVisualizer React component that renders tree structures with a top-down hierarchical layout. Supports node circles, edge lines, subtree collapsing, and smooth rotation/rebalance animations.
acceptanceCriteria: Tree nodes render as circles with value labels in a top-down hierarchical layout
Edges are drawn as lines/curves connecting parent to children
Rotation and rebalance operations animate smoothly (nodes glide to new positions)

title: Implement BST insert/search/delete step generator
description: Create generators for Binary Search Tree operations. Insert traces the path down to the insertion point, search highlights the comparison path, and delete handles all three cases (leaf, one child, two children with in-order successor).
acceptanceCriteria: Insert generator yields comparison steps down to the leaf position and the final node placement
Search generator highlights the traversal path and reports found/not-found
Delete generator handles leaf, single-child, and two-child (in-order successor swap) cases

title: Implement AVL Tree step generator with rotation visualization
description: Create a generator for AVL Tree operations. Steps visualize insert/delete with balance factor computation, detection of imbalance, and the four rotation cases (LL, RR, LR, RL) animated as tree structure transforms.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator detects imbalance after insert/delete and selects correct rotation (LL, RR, LR, RL)
Rotation steps include before/after tree states for smooth animation interpolation
Balance factors are displayed on each node during the rebalancing steps

title: Implement Red-Black Tree step generator with color-flip animation
description: Create a generator for Red-Black Tree operations. Steps visualize insertion with color assignment, uncle checks, color flips, and rotations, all preserving the five Red-Black properties.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator inserts nodes as red and fixes violations via recoloring and rotations
Steps animate color flips (red-to-black and black-to-red) on nodes and uncle/parent
All five Red-Black Tree properties are maintained after every operation

title: Implement B-Tree step generator with page split/merge animation
description: Create a generator for B-Tree operations with configurable order. Steps visualize key insertion into nodes, overflow detection, page splitting (promoting the median), and merge/redistribution on underflow during deletion.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator supports configurable B-Tree order (minimum degree t)
Insert steps show key placement, overflow detection, and upward median promotion split
Delete steps handle redistribution from siblings and merge with parent key

title: Implement Trie insert/search step generator
description: Create a generator for Trie operations. Steps visualize character-by-character traversal during insert and search, node creation for new characters, and end-of-word marking.
acceptanceCriteria: Insert generator creates nodes character-by-character and marks end-of-word
Search generator highlights the traversal path and reports found/not-found/prefix-only
Steps include the current character being processed and the path so far

title: Implement Segment Tree build/query step generator
description: Create a generator for Segment Tree operations. Steps visualize bottom-up tree construction from an array, range query traversal (identifying which segments cover the query range), and point/range updates propagating up the tree.
acceptanceCriteria: Build generator constructs the segment tree bottom-up with merge steps at each internal node
Query generator highlights the O(log n) segments that cover the query range
Update generator propagates changes from leaf to root

title: Implement Heap operations step generator with tree + array dual view
description: Create a generator for binary heap operations (insert, extractMin/Max, heapify). Steps provide dual-view data: both the tree-structure representation and the underlying array, synchronized so each sift-up or sift-down move shows both views updating.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields steps with both tree-node positions and array-index positions
Insert shows sift-up with parent comparisons in both views simultaneously
ExtractMin/Max shows root removal, last-element swap, and sift-down in both views

title: Build DP table visualization renderer with dependency arrows
description: Create a DPTableVisualizer React component that renders a 2D grid (or 1D array) for dynamic programming. Cells highlight as they are computed, with arrows drawn from dependency cells to the current cell to show the recurrence relation.
acceptanceCriteria: Renders a 2D grid with row/column headers configurable per DP problem
Cells animate fill-in order with color transitions (uncomputed, computing, computed)
Dependency arrows drawn from source cells to current cell showing the recurrence

title: Implement Fibonacci DP step generator (top-down vs bottom-up)
description: Create a dual generator for Fibonacci: top-down with memoization (showing recursive call tree and cache hits) and bottom-up tabulation (showing linear table fill). Supports side-by-side comparison of both approaches.
acceptanceCriteria: Top-down generator yields recursive call steps with memoization cache-hit indicators
Bottom-up generator yields linear left-to-right table fill steps
Both generators produce matching results; step metadata includes call count for comparison

title: Implement Longest Common Subsequence DP step generator
description: Create a generator for LCS. Steps visualize the 2D DP table being filled with match/no-match decisions, cell-value computation from diag/left/up neighbors, and the backtracking phase reconstructing the LCS string.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator fills the (m+1)x(n+1) table with steps showing character comparison and cell computation
Match steps highlight the diagonal dependency; no-match steps show max(left, up)
Backtracking phase traces the LCS from the bottom-right cell back to origin

title: Implement Edit Distance (Levenshtein) DP step generator
description: Create a generator for Edit Distance. Steps visualize the 2D DP table fill with three operation choices (insert, delete, substitute) at each cell, the minimum selection, and backtracking to reconstruct the edit sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator fills the (m+1)x(n+1) table with steps showing insert/delete/substitute costs
Each step highlights the three candidate cells and the chosen minimum
Backtracking phase reconstructs the sequence of edit operations

title: Implement 0/1 Knapsack DP step generator
description: Create a generator for the 0/1 Knapsack problem. Steps visualize the 2D DP table (items x capacity) being filled with include/exclude decisions, the optimal value computation, and backtracking to find selected items.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator fills the (n+1)x(W+1) table with include/exclude comparison steps per item per capacity
Steps highlight which items are being considered and the weight/value tradeoff
Backtracking phase identifies the selected items that achieve the optimal value

title: Implement Coin Change DP step generator
description: Create a generator for the Coin Change problem. Steps visualize the 1D DP table being filled from amount 0 to target, trying each coin denomination at each amount, and backtracking to recover the coin combination used.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator fills the dp[0..amount] array trying each coin at each sub-amount
Steps highlight the current amount, the coin being tried, and the dp[amount - coin] lookup
Backtracking phase reconstructs which coins were chosen for the optimal solution

title: Build string match visualization renderer
description: Create a StringMatchVisualizer React component that renders two aligned rows: the text string on top and the pattern string below, with the pattern sliding along the text. Supports character-level highlighting for matches, mismatches, and skip regions.
acceptanceCriteria: Text row and pattern row render as aligned character cells with the pattern at the current offset
Character cells color-code match (green), mismatch (red), and unchecked (gray)
Pattern slides smoothly to the next alignment position on each step

title: Implement KMP string matching step generator
description: Create a generator for the Knuth-Morris-Pratt algorithm. Steps visualize the failure function (prefix table) construction phase and the main matching phase where the pattern advances using the failure function to skip redundant comparisons.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields steps for failure function construction showing prefix/suffix matching
Matching phase steps show character comparison, match extension, and failure-function jumps
Pattern never backtracks over the text; skips are visualized as the pattern sliding forward

title: Implement Rabin-Karp string matching step generator
description: Create a generator for Rabin-Karp. Steps visualize the rolling hash computation for each window, hash comparison with the pattern hash, and full character-by-character verification on hash matches (including spurious hits).
acceptanceCriteria: Generator yields steps showing rolling hash computation (add new char, remove old char)
Hash match triggers a verification phase with character-by-character comparison steps
Spurious hash collisions are visualized as failed verifications

title: Build side-by-side algorithm comparison mode
description: Create a split-view layout that runs two algorithms simultaneously on the same input data. Each side has its own PlaybackController, visualizer, and step counter. A shared timeline allows synchronized or independent playback.
acceptanceCriteria: Split-view renders two algorithm visualizers side by side with identical input data
Each side has independent play/pause/step controls
A 'sync playback' toggle locks both sides to advance steps together at the same speed

title: Build Monaco code panel with line highlighting synced to steps
description: Integrate a Monaco Editor (read-only) in the bottom panel that displays the algorithm's source code. The current line of execution is highlighted and scrolled into view as each animation step plays, providing a code-level walkthrough.
acceptanceCriteria: Monaco Editor renders in read-only mode with syntax highlighting for TypeScript/pseudocode
Current execution line is highlighted (yellow background) and auto-scrolled into view
Line highlight updates in sync with PlaybackController step changes

title: Add custom input support for all algorithm categories
description: Add an input editor to the sidebar that allows users to provide custom input data for any algorithm category: comma-separated arrays for sorting, adjacency list/matrix for graphs, key sequence for trees, string pair for string matching, and problem parameters for DP.
acceptanceCriteria: Sorting accepts comma-separated integers or a random-generate button with size slider
Graph accepts adjacency list text format or the visual graph editor (ALG-022)
DP and string algorithms accept their respective problem-specific inputs (strings, weights, etc.)

title: Create DataStructuresModule shell with sidebar DS selector
description: Build the DataStructuresModule top-level component with the four-panel layout: sidebar listing all data structures grouped by category, main canvas for the active DS visualizer, properties panel for complexity info, and operation log panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar lists all data structures grouped by category (Linear, Tree, Hash, Probabilistic, Distributed)
Clicking a DS in the sidebar loads its visualizer in the main canvas
Properties panel shows time complexity table for the selected DS operations

title: Define DS types (DSConfig, DSOperation, DSAnimationStep)
description: Create the core type system for the Data Structure Explorer: DSConfig (structure metadata, supported operations, complexity), DSOperation (operation name, args, result), and DSAnimationStep (visual mutations specific to DS interactions).
acceptanceCriteria: DSConfig type includes name, category, supportedOperations, and complexity table
DSOperation type captures operation name, arguments, return value, and step sequence
DSAnimationStep extends the base animation step with DS-specific mutations (pointer-move, node-create, node-delete, relink)

title: Implement Reingold-Tilford tree layout engine
description: Build a Reingold-Tilford tree layout algorithm that computes x,y positions for tree nodes with minimal width and aesthetic symmetry. Used by all tree-based DS visualizers for consistent, compact layouts.
acceptanceCriteria: Algorithm computes node positions that minimize total tree width
Subtrees are symmetric: mirrored trees produce mirrored layouts
Layout updates incrementally when nodes are added or removed without full recomputation

title: Build ArrayDSVisualizer (Array, Stack, Queue, Deque, Circular Buffer)
description: Create a unified linear DS visualizer for array-based structures. Renders indexed cells with push/pop/enqueue/dequeue animations, head/tail pointers for queues, and a circular layout mode for circular buffers.
acceptanceCriteria: Renders array cells with index labels and value display
Stack operations animate push (slide-in from top) and pop (slide-out from top)
Queue/Deque show head and tail pointers; Circular Buffer wraps around visually in a ring layout

title: Build LinkedListVisualizer (Singly, Doubly, Circular)
description: Create a visualizer for linked list variants. Nodes render as boxes with value + pointer arrow(s). Supports insert, delete, search, and reverse operations with animated pointer manipulation.
acceptanceCriteria: Nodes render as boxes connected by directional arrows (single arrow for singly, bidirectional for doubly)
Insert/delete operations animate pointer re-linking with arrows bending to new targets
Circular variant connects the tail back to the head with a visible wrap-around arrow

title: Build HashTableVisualizer (separate chaining + open addressing)
description: Create a hash table visualizer supporting separate chaining (bucket array with linked lists) and open addressing (linear probing, quadratic probing, double hashing). Shows hash computation, collision handling, and load factor gauge.
acceptanceCriteria: Separate chaining mode renders bucket array with linked-list chains per bucket
Open addressing mode highlights probe sequence (linear, quadratic, or double hash) on collision
Load factor gauge updates on insert/delete and triggers resize animation at threshold

title: Build BST interactive operations visualizer
description: Create an interactive BST explorer where users can type values to insert, search, or delete. Each operation plays through the step generator with animated node highlighting, pointer traversal, and tree restructuring.
acceptanceCriteria: Input field accepts a value and operation buttons (Insert, Search, Delete) trigger the corresponding animation
Insert animates path traversal to the leaf and new node appearance
Delete handles all three cases with animated node removal and successor replacement

title: Build AVL Tree interactive visualizer with rotation animation
description: Create an interactive AVL Tree explorer. Operations animate the BST path traversal followed by balance-factor checks and smooth rotation animations (LL, RR, LR, RL) when rebalancing is needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Balance factors are displayed on each node and update after every operation
Rotation animations show nodes smoothly gliding to their new positions
Supports insert and delete with automatic rebalancing

title: Build Red-Black Tree interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Red-Black Tree explorer. Nodes are colored red/black, and operations animate the fixup procedure: color flips, rotations, and property restoration.
acceptanceCriteria: Nodes render with red or black fill matching their color property
Insert fixup animates uncle checks, color flips, and rotations step by step
All five Red-Black properties are displayed as a checklist that updates after each operation

title: Build B-Tree interactive visualizer with configurable order
description: Create an interactive B-Tree explorer with configurable order (minimum degree t). Visualizes multi-key nodes, overflow splits with median promotion, and underflow merges/redistribution.
acceptanceCriteria: Order slider lets users configure minimum degree t from 2 to 5
Node boxes render multiple keys with separator lines between them
Split animation shows the median key rising to the parent while siblings separate

title: Build B+ Tree interactive visualizer with leaf linked list
description: Create an interactive B+ Tree explorer. Extends the B-Tree visualizer with all values stored in leaves, internal nodes as index-only guides, and a visible linked list connecting leaf nodes for range queries.
acceptanceCriteria: Internal nodes contain only separator keys; all actual values reside in leaf nodes
Leaf nodes are connected by a horizontal linked-list arrow for range traversal
Range query operation highlights the leaf linked-list path from start to end key

title: Build Trie interactive visualizer with prefix search and autocomplete
description: Create an interactive Trie explorer. Users type strings to insert, search for prefixes, and see autocomplete suggestions. Nodes render as circles with single characters, and the current traversal path is highlighted.
acceptanceCriteria: Insert operation animates character-by-character node creation along the path
Prefix search highlights the matching path and lists all words sharing that prefix
End-of-word nodes are visually distinct (double circle or filled dot)

title: Build Segment Tree interactive visualizer with lazy propagation
description: Create an interactive Segment Tree explorer. Users can build from an array, query ranges, and update points/ranges. Lazy propagation is visualized with pending-update markers on internal nodes that flush on access.
acceptanceCriteria: Build operation animates bottom-up construction from the input array
Range query highlights the O(log n) segments that cover the query range
Lazy propagation markers appear on nodes with pending updates and animate flushing downward on access

title: Build Fenwick Tree (BIT) interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Fenwick Tree (Binary Indexed Tree) explorer. Visualizes the underlying array alongside the BIT array, showing which original indices each BIT cell is responsible for. Update and prefix-sum queries animate the index-hopping pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Dual-row display shows the original array and the BIT array with responsibility ranges
Update operation animates the i += (i & -i) index-hop pattern up through the BIT
Prefix sum query animates the i -= (i & -i) index-hop pattern accumulating partial sums

title: Build Skip List interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Skip List explorer. Renders multiple linked-list levels with express lanes. Insert animates the coin-flip level assignment, tower construction, and pointer threading. Search shows the top-down, right-then-down traversal pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Multi-level structure renders with horizontal arrows at each level and vertical towers for promoted nodes
Insert animates coin flips determining the tower height, then threads pointers at each level
Search animates the express-lane traversal: move right while possible, then drop down a level

title: Build Bloom Filter interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Bloom Filter explorer. Visualizes the bit array, k hash function outputs mapping to bit positions, and a false positive rate gauge. Users can insert items and test membership with clear true-positive vs false-positive feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Bit array renders as a row of cells that toggle from 0 to 1 when hashed positions are set
Insert shows k hash function outputs as arrows from the item to k bit positions
False positive rate gauge updates after each insert based on fill ratio and k

title: Build Count-Min Sketch interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Count-Min Sketch explorer. Visualizes the d x w matrix of counters, d hash functions mapping items to column positions in each row, and the minimum-of-rows frequency estimate.
acceptanceCriteria: D x W counter matrix renders as a grid with row labels (hash functions) and column indices
Insert operation highlights the hashed column in each row and increments those counters
Query shows the minimum across all d rows as the estimated frequency

title: Build HyperLogLog interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive HyperLogLog explorer. Visualizes hash computation, bucket assignment from leading bits, trailing-zero counting, and cardinality estimation using the harmonic mean formula. Shows the accuracy improving as more items are added.
acceptanceCriteria: Renders m register buckets with their current max trailing-zero values
Insert shows the hash binary representation, bucket selection bits, and trailing-zero count
Cardinality estimate updates after each insert with an accuracy error bar visualization

title: Build LSM Tree interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive LSM Tree explorer. Visualizes the memtable (in-memory sorted structure), immutable memtable flush to SSTable on disk, level-based compaction merging SSTables, and read path checking memtable then L0 then L1 etc.
acceptanceCriteria: Memtable renders as a sorted in-memory structure that accepts writes
Flush operation animates the memtable becoming immutable and writing to L0 as an SSTable
Compaction animation merges overlapping SSTables from level N into level N+1

title: Build Heap interactive visualizer with tree + array dual view
description: Create an interactive Heap (min/max/priority queue) explorer. Renders both the tree visualization and the underlying array simultaneously. Operations (insert, extractMin, decreaseKey) animate in both views in sync.
acceptanceCriteria: Dual view shows the binary tree structure and the flat array with parent-child index relationships
Insert animates sift-up in both tree (node rising) and array (element moving left) views
Toggle between min-heap and max-heap modes with the heap property label updating

title: Build Disjoint Set (Union-Find) interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Union-Find explorer. Visualizes the forest of trees representing disjoint sets. Union operations merge trees (with union by rank), and find operations show path traversal with optional path compression flattening the tree.
acceptanceCriteria: Forest of trees renders with each tree rooted at the set representative
Union by rank attaches the shorter tree under the taller tree's root
Path compression animates nodes relinking directly to the root during find

title: Build Consistent Hash Ring interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Consistent Hash Ring explorer. Renders a circular ring with node positions, virtual nodes, and key placements. Users can add/remove nodes and see key redistribution with minimal movement highlighted.
acceptanceCriteria: Circular ring renders with server nodes and their virtual node replicas at hashed positions
Keys map to the ring and visually walk clockwise to their assigned node
Adding/removing a node animates key redistribution showing which keys move and which stay

title: Build Merkle Tree interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Merkle Tree explorer. Visualizes bottom-up hash construction from data blocks, and the verification path showing which sibling hashes are needed to prove a leaf's inclusion without revealing other data.
acceptanceCriteria: Leaf nodes display data block hashes; internal nodes display hash(left||right)
Build animation shows bottom-up hash computation propagating to the root
Verification path for a selected leaf highlights the sibling hashes needed for proof

title: Build CRDTs interactive visualizer (G-Counter, PN-Counter, LWW-Register, OR-Set)
description: Create an interactive CRDT explorer with 3 replica panels. Each replica independently performs operations, and a merge button shows convergence. Supports G-Counter, PN-Counter, LWW-Register, and OR-Set with visual state diffs.
acceptanceCriteria: Three replica panels each show the local CRDT state and accept independent operations
Merge operation between any two replicas animates state transfer and convergence
All four CRDT types (G-Counter, PN-Counter, LWW-Register, OR-Set) are selectable

title: Build Vector Clock interactive visualizer with space-time diagram
description: Create an interactive Vector Clock explorer with a space-time diagram. Vertical timelines represent processes, horizontal position represents time, and diagonal arrows show message passing with vector clock updates at each event.
acceptanceCriteria: Space-time diagram renders vertical process timelines with event dots
Local events increment the process's own clock; send/receive events update via max-merge
Diagonal arrows between timelines show message passing with vector clock values at each endpoint

title: Build Splay Tree interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Splay Tree explorer. Operations splay the accessed node to the root via zig, zig-zig, and zig-zag rotations, with smooth animations showing the node rising through the tree structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Access, insert, and delete operations splay the target node to the root
Zig, zig-zig, and zig-zag rotation patterns are animated with nodes gliding to new positions
Tree depth and amortized cost indicator update after each operation

title: Build Treap interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Treap explorer. Each node has a key (BST property) and a random priority (heap property). Insert assigns a random priority and rotates to maintain heap order. Visualizes the dual BST+heap invariant.
acceptanceCriteria: Nodes display both key and priority values
Insert assigns a random priority and rotates the node up until heap order is restored
BST in-order property and min-heap priority property are both visually verifiable

title: Build R-Tree interactive visualizer with bounding rectangles
description: Create an interactive R-Tree explorer for spatial indexing. Visualizes 2D bounding rectangles at each level of the tree, with insert showing rectangle expansion, and search showing the pruning of non-overlapping branches.
acceptanceCriteria: 2D canvas renders nested bounding rectangles color-coded by tree level
Insert shows the chosen subtree path and bounding rectangle expansion
Range search query highlights overlapping rectangles and prunes non-overlapping branches

title: Build Quadtree interactive visualizer with 2D subdivision
description: Create an interactive Quadtree explorer. Users click to place points on a 2D canvas. The quadtree subdivides regions into four quadrants when capacity is exceeded, with subdivision lines animated.
acceptanceCriteria: 2D canvas accepts click-to-place points
Region subdivides into four quadrants (NW, NE, SW, SE) when capacity threshold is exceeded
Range query highlights the quadrants that overlap the query rectangle and returns matching points

title: Build Rope interactive visualizer for text editing
description: Create an interactive Rope data structure explorer. Visualizes the balanced binary tree of string fragments. Insert, delete, and concat operations animate tree splits, joins, and rebalancing with the full string rendered below.
acceptanceCriteria: Tree nodes display string fragments at leaves and weight (left subtree length) at internal nodes
Insert/delete operations animate split and join with tree restructuring
Full concatenated string is rendered below the tree, updating in sync with operations

title: Build Fibonacci Heap interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Fibonacci Heap explorer. Visualizes the circular root list of heap-ordered trees, lazy merging, cascading cuts on decrease-key, and the consolidation pass after extract-min.
acceptanceCriteria: Circular root list renders as horizontally linked trees with the min pointer highlighted
Decrease-key animates the cut, potential cascading cuts, and insertion into root list
Extract-min animates min removal, child promotion to root list, and consolidation by degree

title: Build Binomial Heap interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Binomial Heap explorer. Visualizes the linked list of binomial trees ordered by degree, merge operations combining same-degree trees, and extract-min with tree decomposition.
acceptanceCriteria: Root list renders binomial trees of increasing degree left to right
Merge operation animates combining two heaps by linking same-degree trees
Extract-min removes the min-root tree, decomposes its children back into the root list, and re-merges

title: Build WAL (Write-Ahead Log) interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Write-Ahead Log explorer. Visualizes the sequential append-only log, checkpointing, and crash recovery. Users trigger writes, checkpoints, and simulated crashes, then watch the recovery replay from the last checkpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: Log renders as a sequential tape of entries with LSN (Log Sequence Number) labels
Checkpoint operation marks a position and flushes dirty pages to disk (visualized)
Crash + recovery replays log entries from the last checkpoint, re-applying committed transactions

title: Build Persistent Red-Black Tree interactive visualizer
description: Create an interactive Persistent (immutable) Red-Black Tree explorer. Insert creates a new version by path-copying the affected nodes while sharing unchanged subtrees. Users can browse version history and see structural sharing.
acceptanceCriteria: Insert creates a new tree version via path-copying with shared subtrees highlighted
Version timeline allows selecting any past version to view its tree state
Shared (unchanged) subtrees are visually dimmed while path-copied nodes are highlighted

title: Build properties panel with complexity table and operation log
description: Create a shared properties panel component for all DS visualizers. Displays a complexity table (average/worst for each operation), a scrollable operation log showing every user action and its result, and current structure statistics (size, height, load factor, etc.).
acceptanceCriteria: Complexity table renders average and worst-case time for each supported operation
Operation log scrolls and shows timestamped entries of every user action and its outcome
Structure statistics (size, height, load factor, etc.) update in real time after each operation

title: Implement Raft consensus full simulation
description: Build the complete Raft consensus simulation engine: leader election (with random election timeouts), log replication (AppendEntries RPCs), commit index advancement, and crash/recover/partition fault injection.
acceptanceCriteria: Leader election works with configurable election timeouts and handles split-vote scenarios
Log replication via AppendEntries RPCs correctly propagates entries and handles conflicts
Crash, recover, and network partition can be injected at any time during simulation

title: Build Raft SVG visualization (pentagon layout, role colors, event log)
description: Create the Raft visualizer component: 5-node pentagon layout, nodes colored by role (leader=gold, follower=blue, candidate=orange), animated heartbeat pulses, and a scrollable event log showing RPCs and state transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Five nodes arranged in a pentagon with role-based color coding
Heartbeat RPCs animate as pulses along edges from leader to followers
Event log panel displays timestamped entries for elections, RPCs, and state changes

title: Implement consistent hashing ring simulation
description: Build the consistent hashing simulation engine with FNV-1a hashing, configurable virtual nodes per physical node, and key-to-node assignment via clockwise walk. Supports add/remove node with minimal key redistribution tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: FNV-1a hash function maps nodes and keys to positions on the ring
Virtual nodes are configurable (1-256 per physical node) and evenly distribute load
Add/remove node correctly redistributes only affected keys with tracking of moved keys

title: Build consistent hashing SVG ring visualization
description: Create the Consistent Hash Ring visualizer: circular ring with server node markers, virtual node indicators, key dots walking clockwise to their assigned server, and redistribution animation on node add/remove.
acceptanceCriteria: Circular SVG ring renders with server nodes at their hashed positions
Virtual nodes shown as smaller markers clustered near their physical node
Key dots animate clockwise walk to the first server node they encounter

title: Implement vector clock simulation engine
description: Build the vector clock simulation with N processes supporting localEvent (increment own clock), sendEvent (increment and attach vector), and receiveEvent (element-wise max merge). Includes happens-before causality queries.
acceptanceCriteria: localEvent increments the process's own clock component
sendEvent increments own clock and attaches the full vector to the message
receiveEvent merges via element-wise max and then increments own component

title: Implement gossip protocol simulation engine
description: Build the gossip protocol (epidemic) simulation: configurable fanout, push-based gossip rounds, infection state tracking (susceptible/infected/removed), and convergence measurement (percentage of nodes with the data).
acceptanceCriteria: Each round, infected nodes randomly select 'fanout' peers to push data to
Nodes transition through susceptible, infected, and removed states
Convergence percentage is tracked per round and reaches 100% within expected O(log N) rounds

title: Implement CRDT types (G-Counter, PN-Counter, LWW-Register, OR-Set)
description: Build four CRDT implementations: G-Counter (grow-only), PN-Counter (positive-negative), LWW-Register (last-writer-wins with timestamp), and OR-Set (observed-remove set with unique tags). Each supports merge for convergence.
acceptanceCriteria: G-Counter supports increment and merge with element-wise max
PN-Counter supports increment/decrement and merge via two G-Counters
LWW-Register and OR-Set converge correctly after concurrent updates and merges

title: Implement CAP theorem explorer simulation
description: Build the CAP theorem explorer: simulates a 3-node distributed store where users can toggle network partitions and observe the system choosing consistency (reject writes during partition) or availability (accept writes, risk divergence).
acceptanceCriteria: Three nodes render with read/write operations and configurable CP or AP mode
Network partition toggle isolates one node and shows the tradeoff in real time
CP mode rejects writes during partition; AP mode accepts writes and shows divergence

title: Build DistributedModule with all 6 simulations in sidebar
description: Create the DistributedModule top-level component wiring the sidebar (listing Raft, Consistent Hashing, Vector Clocks, Gossip, CRDTs, CAP Theorem) to the main canvas area that swaps in the appropriate visualizer.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar lists all 6 distributed system simulations with icons
Selecting a simulation loads its visualizer and controls in the main canvas
Module layout matches the standard four-panel pattern (sidebar, canvas, properties, bottom)

title: Build Vector Clocks space-time diagram visualization
description: Create an SVG space-time diagram for Vector Clocks. Vertical timelines per process, event dots with vector clock labels, diagonal arrows for message send/receive, and happens-before relationship highlighting on click.
acceptanceCriteria: Vertical timelines render per process with event dots at each local/send/receive event
Diagonal arrows connect send events to receive events across process timelines
Clicking two events highlights whether they have a happens-before relationship or are concurrent

title: Build Gossip Protocol epidemic spread visualization
description: Create a 2D node-map visualization for the Gossip Protocol. Nodes are scattered on a 2D plane, colored by infection state (gray=susceptible, orange=infected, green=removed). An S-curve convergence chart tracks the percentage of infected nodes over rounds.
acceptanceCriteria: 2D plane renders nodes colored by infection state with animated state transitions
Gossip messages animate as particles flying between sender and selected peers each round
S-curve convergence chart plots the cumulative infection percentage per round

title: Build CRDTs visualization with 3 replica panels
description: Create a CRDT visualizer with three side-by-side replica panels. Each panel shows the local CRDT state, accepts operations, and displays the internal state (vector, timestamps, tags). Merge arrows animate state transfer between replicas with convergence verification.
acceptanceCriteria: Three replica panels render side by side, each showing local CRDT state and operation buttons
Merge operation animates an arrow carrying state from source to target replica
After all merges, a convergence check confirms all replicas have identical values

title: Build CAP Theorem triangle diagram with scenario toggles
description: Create a CAP Theorem visualizer with the classic triangle (Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance). A draggable point or toggle buttons let users select CP, AP, or CA modes, with scenario descriptions and real-world system examples for each.
acceptanceCriteria: CAP triangle renders with labeled vertices (C, A, P) and shaded regions for CP, AP, CA
Toggle buttons switch between CP, AP, and CA modes with a scenario description panel
Each mode lists 2-3 real-world system examples (e.g., CP: HBase, ZooKeeper)

title: Implement Paxos protocol simulation engine
description: Build a Multi-Paxos simulation engine with Proposer, Acceptor, and Learner roles. Supports Prepare/Promise and Accept/Accepted phases, handles dueling proposers, and tracks the decided value.
acceptanceCriteria: Proposer sends Prepare(n) and collects Promise responses with any previously accepted values
Accept phase uses the highest-numbered accepted value or the proposer's own value
Dueling proposers scenario correctly resolves with one value eventually decided

title: Build Paxos visualization (Prepare/Promise/Accept/Accepted phases)
description: Create a Paxos visualizer showing Proposer, Acceptor, and Learner nodes in a column layout. Animated message arrows flow between roles for each phase (Prepare, Promise, Accept, Accepted), with proposal numbers and values labeled on each message.
acceptanceCriteria: Three-column layout renders Proposers, Acceptors, and Learners with role labels
Message arrows animate between columns for each phase with proposal number and value labels
Phase timeline at the bottom shows the progression: Prepare, Promise, Accept, Accepted, Decided

title: Implement Lamport Timestamps with Vector Clock comparison
description: Build a Lamport Timestamp simulation alongside Vector Clocks for comparison. Shows where Lamport timestamps fail to capture concurrency (false positives in happens-before) while Vector Clocks correctly detect concurrent events.
acceptanceCriteria: Lamport timestamp simulation increments on local events and takes max+1 on receive
Side-by-side comparison with Vector Clocks highlights events where Lamport timestamps give false happens-before ordering
Concurrent event pairs are labeled correctly by Vector Clocks but incorrectly ordered by Lamport timestamps

title: Build Two-Phase Commit visualization
description: Create a Two-Phase Commit (2PC) visualizer with a coordinator and N participant nodes. Phase 1 sends Prepare and collects votes (Yes/No). Phase 2 sends Commit or Abort based on votes. Handles coordinator crash and participant timeout scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: Coordinator node sends Prepare arrows to all participants in Phase 1
Participant votes (Yes/No) animate back; Phase 2 sends Commit (all Yes) or Abort (any No)
Coordinator crash scenario shows participants stuck in uncertain state with timeout recovery

title: Build Saga Pattern visualization (choreography + orchestration)
description: Create a Saga Pattern visualizer showing both choreography (event-driven, no coordinator) and orchestration (central saga coordinator) modes. Each step is a local transaction with a compensating transaction on failure, visualized as a pipeline with rollback arrows.
acceptanceCriteria: Choreography mode shows services publishing events to trigger the next step with no central coordinator
Orchestration mode shows a central coordinator dispatching commands to each service in sequence
Failure at any step triggers compensating transactions rolling back in reverse order

title: Build MapReduce visualization (word count pipeline)
description: Create a MapReduce visualizer using the classic word-count example. Shows the input split, map phase (emit key-value pairs), shuffle/sort (group by key), and reduce phase (aggregate counts). Data flows visually from left to right through each phase.
acceptanceCriteria: Input documents split into chunks assigned to mapper nodes
Map phase emits (word, 1) pairs with animated output from each mapper
Shuffle groups pairs by key, and reduce phase sums counts with the final output table

title: Add Raft message animation (RPCs as animated arrows between nodes)
description: Enhance the existing Raft visualizer with animated message arrows for every RPC: RequestVote, AppendEntries, and their responses. Arrows carry payload labels (term, log index, success/reject) and travel along the pentagon edges with configurable speed.
acceptanceCriteria: RequestVote RPCs animate as arrows from candidate to all other nodes with term label
AppendEntries RPCs animate from leader to followers with entry count and prevLogIndex
Response arrows animate back with success/reject status and matching term

title: Implement TCP state machine simulation
description: Build the TCP state machine simulation engine modeling all 11 TCP states (CLOSED, LISTEN, SYN_SENT, SYN_RECEIVED, ESTABLISHED, FIN_WAIT_1, FIN_WAIT_2, CLOSE_WAIT, CLOSING, LAST_ACK, TIME_WAIT) with transitions triggered by client/server events.
acceptanceCriteria: All 11 TCP states are modeled with correct transition rules
Three-way handshake (SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK) transitions through correct states
Four-way teardown (FIN, ACK, FIN, ACK) transitions through correct states including TIME_WAIT

title: Implement TLS 1.3 handshake simulation (1-RTT, 0-RTT, TLS 1.2 comparison)
description: Build the TLS handshake simulation engine supporting TLS 1.3 full handshake (1-RTT), TLS 1.3 resumption (0-RTT), and TLS 1.2 handshake for comparison. Models key exchange, certificate verification, and encrypted data start.
acceptanceCriteria: TLS 1.3 full handshake completes in 1-RTT with key exchange and certificate in the first flight
TLS 1.3 0-RTT resumption sends early data with the ClientHello using a PSK
TLS 1.2 comparison shows the extra round-trip required for ServerKeyExchange

title: Implement DNS resolution flow simulation
description: Build the DNS resolution simulation engine modeling the full recursive resolution chain: client cache check, recursive resolver, root nameserver, TLD nameserver, authoritative nameserver. Supports CNAME chains and TTL-based caching.
acceptanceCriteria: Resolution chain follows client cache, recursive resolver, root, TLD, authoritative flow
CNAME records trigger additional resolution chains for the canonical name
TTL-based caching at each level avoids redundant lookups on repeated queries

title: Implement HTTP/1.1 vs 2 vs 3 comparison simulation
description: Build a simulation engine comparing HTTP/1.1 (serial requests, head-of-line blocking), HTTP/2 (multiplexed streams, header compression, server push), and HTTP/3 (QUIC-based, no TCP HOL blocking). Models concurrent requests and measures total load time.
acceptanceCriteria: HTTP/1.1 simulation shows serial request queuing and head-of-line blocking
HTTP/2 simulation shows multiplexed streams over a single connection with HPACK compression
HTTP/3 simulation shows QUIC streams with independent loss recovery per stream

title: Implement WebSocket lifecycle simulation
description: Build the WebSocket lifecycle simulation: HTTP upgrade handshake, bidirectional frame exchange, ping/pong keepalive, and graceful close with close frames. Models both client-initiated and server-initiated messages.
acceptanceCriteria: HTTP upgrade handshake (101 Switching Protocols) initiates the WebSocket connection
Bidirectional text/binary frames flow between client and server with frame metadata
Graceful close sequence with Close frames and status codes from either side

title: Implement CORS flow simulation
description: Build the CORS flow simulator modeling simple requests (no preflight), preflighted requests (OPTIONS + actual), and credentialed requests. Shows which headers trigger preflight and how Access-Control-* response headers control access.
acceptanceCriteria: Simple request flow skips preflight for safe methods with simple headers
Preflight flow sends OPTIONS with Access-Control-Request-* headers and checks the response
Credentialed request flow requires Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true and explicit origin

title: Build NetworkingModule with protocol selector and SequenceDiagram component
description: Create the NetworkingModule top-level component with a sidebar listing all protocols and a reusable SequenceDiagram component for rendering client-server message flows. The sequence diagram supports animated arrows, labeled messages, and timing annotations.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar lists all 6 protocol simulations with icons and descriptions
SequenceDiagram component renders participant columns, labeled message arrows, and self-messages
Selecting a protocol loads its simulation and renders steps in the SequenceDiagram

title: Build TCP handshake sequence diagram visualization
description: Create the TCP handshake visualization using the SequenceDiagram component. Shows the 3-way handshake (SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK), data transfer phase, and 4-way teardown with TCP state labels on each participant at each step.
acceptanceCriteria: Client and Server columns with TCP state labels updating at each step
Three-way handshake arrows (SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK) animate with sequence numbers
Four-way teardown arrows (FIN, ACK, FIN, ACK) show the TIME_WAIT state on the initiator

title: Build TLS 1.3 handshake visualization
description: Create a TLS 1.3 visualization using the SequenceDiagram component. Shows the 1-RTT flow with ClientHello (key share), ServerHello (key share + encrypted extensions + certificate + finished), and client Finished. Color-codes plaintext vs encrypted message regions.
acceptanceCriteria: Sequence diagram shows Client and Server with messages labeled by TLS record type
Messages after ServerHello are rendered in a shaded 'encrypted' region to show when encryption begins
0-RTT mode shows early data sent alongside ClientHello with a warning about replay risk

title: Build DNS resolution chain visualization
description: Create a multi-column DNS resolution visualization: Client, Recursive Resolver, Root Nameserver, TLD Nameserver, and Authoritative Nameserver. Query and response arrows flow between columns with DNS record types, TTLs, and cache-hit indicators.
acceptanceCriteria: Five columns render for Client, Recursive Resolver, Root, TLD, and Authoritative nameservers
Query arrows flow right and response arrows flow left with record type and TTL labels
Cache hits at any level short-circuit the chain with a 'cached' badge on the response

title: Build HTTP/1.1 vs 2 vs 3 side-by-side waterfall visualization
description: Create a three-column comparison visualization for HTTP versions. Each column shows a waterfall chart of concurrent resource requests: HTTP/1.1 with sequential blocking, HTTP/2 with multiplexed streams, HTTP/3 with QUIC independent streams. Total load time bar at the bottom for comparison.
acceptanceCriteria: Three columns render waterfall charts for HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, and HTTP/3 simultaneously
HTTP/1.1 shows head-of-line blocking with sequential request bars
Total load time bar at the bottom compares the three protocols with percentage improvement labels

title: Build WebSocket lifecycle visualization
description: Create a WebSocket lifecycle visualization using the SequenceDiagram component. Shows the HTTP upgrade handshake, bidirectional data frames (client-to-server and server-to-client interleaved), ping/pong heartbeats, and graceful close sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: HTTP upgrade request/response renders at the top with 101 status code
Bidirectional data frames render as alternating left/right arrows with frame type labels
Close handshake renders with close frames, status code, and reason string

title: Build CORS flow visualization
description: Create a CORS flow visualization using the SequenceDiagram component. Shows Browser, Origin Server, and Cross-Origin Server columns. Renders simple request flow, preflight OPTIONS flow, and credentialed request flow with header annotations on each message.
acceptanceCriteria: Three participant columns: Browser, Origin Server, and Cross-Origin Server
Preflight flow shows OPTIONS request with Access-Control-Request-* headers and the 204 response
Header annotations on each arrow show the relevant CORS headers (Origin, Access-Control-Allow-*)

title: Build CDN request flow visualization
description: Create a CDN request flow visualization showing the User, Edge POP, Origin Shield, and Origin Server. Renders cache-hit path (short-circuited at edge), cache-miss path (full origin fetch), and a geographic world map with POP locations.
acceptanceCriteria: Flow diagram shows User, Edge POP, Origin Shield, and Origin Server with request/response arrows
Cache-hit path terminates at the Edge POP with a green 'HIT' badge; cache-miss continues to origin
Geographic map shows POP locations as dots with the selected edge POP highlighted

title: Build gRPC vs REST vs GraphQL comparison visualization
description: Create a three-column comparison showing the same operation (e.g., fetch user with posts) implemented in gRPC (protobuf), REST (JSON with multiple endpoints), and GraphQL (single query). Shows payload size, request count, and over/under-fetching differences.
acceptanceCriteria: Three columns render the same operation in gRPC, REST, and GraphQL with actual request/response payloads
Payload size comparison bar shows gRPC (protobuf) < REST (JSON) with GraphQL in between
REST column shows multiple requests for related resources; GraphQL shows a single query; gRPC shows a single RPC
